x-enumeration-literals.name: Lessee
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A lessee is a person who rents land or property from a lessor. (Investopedia: https://www.investopedia.com/terms/l/lessee.asp)

The lessee is also known as the tenant and must uphold specific obligations as defined in the lease agreement and by law.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Lessor
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A lessor is the owner of an asset that is leased, or rented, to another party, known as the lessee. (Investopedia: https://www.investopedia.com/terms/l/lessor.asp)

As such, a lessor is the owner of an asset that is leased under an agreement to a lessee. The lessee makes a one-time payment or a series of periodic payments to the lessor in return for the use of the asset.
